title: Private band UI warning
description: No visual confirmation that <!-- private --> bands are working. Users need to see their page has private content. Show warning in rendered page view.

title: v1.5 milestone
description: Near-term features building on v1 foundation: wikilink graph, structured data, API safety, viral growth.

title: D3 force-graph of wikilinks
description: Wikilink model already tracks [[link]] refs between pages. Need: (1) API endpoint to dump graph as JSON (nodes + edges), (2) page with D3 force layout visualization. ~80 lines of D3. High demo value.

title: JSON-LD structured data on pages
description: Add <script type=application/ld+json> block to page template with @type: Article, title, author, dateModified. Trivial effort, free SEO/discoverability.

title: Optimistic locking (If-Match / ETag / 409)
description: Pages have git commit SHAs. Return SHA as ETag on GET, require If-Match on PUT, 409 if stale. Prevents silent data loss from concurrent edits.

title: Twitter auth / tweet-to-verify signup
description: Signup requires tweeting a specific post and pasting tweet URL to verify. Viral distribution + lightweight sybil resistance. Agent-compatible via Twitter API key.

title: Per-wiki llms.txt endpoint
description: /@user/wiki/llms.txt — site-wide exists but per-wiki version is what agents want when working with a specific wiki. Trivial to add.

title: Wikilink rewriting on page rename
description: Rename works but doesn't scan/rewrite [[old-path]] wikilinks. Silent link breakage = data integrity issue. Spec requires atomic rewrite of all referencing pages.

title: v3 milestone
description: Advanced features: realtime collab, org/multi-tenant, encryption, moderation tooling.

title: Paste-unstructured-text → LLM generates wiki
description: Server-hosted coding agent with platform key. Leaning headless Claude Code over raw Anthropic tool-use. Needs agent orchestration layer.

title: Link-share token expiry
description: v1 link tokens are permanent. Add expiry syntax or Postgres-side override.

title: D3 force-graph render of wikilinks (site-wide)
description: Cross-wiki graph visualization. Extends the per-wiki v1.5 graph to full platform view.

title: git-crypt escape hatch for encrypted wikis
description: Encrypted personal wikis pattern from jacobcole.net/labs.

title: Multi-human-one-agent session semantics
description: Multiple humans collaborating through a single agent session.

title: Wikipedia-grade moderation tooling
description: Edit filters, trust levels, auto-revert bots. Full moderation suite.

title: Org / multi-tenant surface
description: Anthropic intranet target. Principal table shape is ready. Multi-tenancy / org support.

title: Signed-in mode, link-share mode ACL
description: Deferred ACL modes: signed-in, link-share, link:token:role grants, group:name:role grants.

title: URL / repo connect (import from public git repos)
description: git clone --bare + walk tree + index into Postgres. Plumbing exists in git_sync.py. Need UI form and background worker.

title: HTML / Google Sites importer
description: Import existing HTML sites/Google Sites into wiki format.

title: Inline diff UI for suggested edits
description: Fork is v1; suggest-edits-back-upstream PR-like mechanic (diff format, list UI, accept/reject, cherry-pick). No current user demand yet.

title: Friends-list / group creation UI
description: Grant syntax is v1, the management UI is v2+. Group CRUD, member management, use in ACL grants.

title: Real-time collaborative editing
description: CRDT/OT. Massive undertaking. Async-first, not biased toward realtime.

title: Server-hosted cloud agent
description: Talk-to-an-agent-we-host via web UI, per-user folder sandbox via UNIX permissions.

title: Featured curation admin surface
description: v1 uses most-starred automatically. Admin override for manual curation.

title: SFTP upload path
description: Session-batched commits, SSH key endpoint, sshfs/rsync compatible. Needs SSH server daemon.

title: v2 milestone
description: Core platform features: abuse protection, social features, content import, polish.

title: IP rate limit on writes (10/min/IP)
description: Anti-abuse priority #1. flask-limiter already referenced in app/__init__.py. Decorator config on write endpoints. Infra-level, not moderation.

title: Comments on pages and wikis
description: ACL vocabulary already reserves 'comment' role. Need Comment model, API endpoints, UI component. Social feature that drives engagement.

title: Omni-convert upload (PDF/DOCX → markdown)
description: Pandoc handles DOCX→MD well. PDF is messier (pdftotext or LLM). Could ship DOCX-only first as quick win — zip upload infra already exists.

title: Landing page live background
description: Featured/popular wiki cards slowly drifting/orbiting behind hero section. Pure CSS animation. Needs enough public wikis to look good — ship after dogfood migrations populate platform.

title: Panic button: disable anonymous writes per wiki
description: Anti-abuse priority #2. Toggle to instantly disable anonymous writes on a wiki. Quick emergency response for spam/abuse.

title: Revert last N anonymous edits button
description: Anti-abuse priority #3. Web UI button to bulk-revert recent anonymous edits. Recovery path after spam attacks.

title: Logout functionality
description: There is no way to log out. Need a logout route that clears the session.

title: MCP server endpoint (/mcp)
description: Discovery endpoints exist (.well-known/mcp/server-card.json, /.well-known/mcp) but the actual /mcp endpoint serving MCP protocol is missing. Tools: whoami, search, read_page, list_pages, create_page, update_page, append_section, delete_page, set_visibility, share, create_wiki, fork_wiki, commit_log. This is the whole thesis — wikihub is GitHub for LLM wikis.
